item.title: Man beaten to death over theft suspicion
item.description: A man was beaten to death by local residents on theft suspicion after he was allegedly caught breaking into a house in Manikganj’s Singair upazila early Monday....

item.title: NEWBORN DEATHS: Govt to decide action against Ad-din today
item.description: A final decision on the consequences that Ad-din Hospital must face is expected today after the hospital authority’s reply to the show-cause notice served on them four days ago....

item.title: Govt plans revised textbooks for 2027
item.description: The government has taken the initiative to revise textbooks for the 2027 academic year, while educationists have criticised successive governments’ practice of including politically biased contents in textbooks, particularly in history textbooks....

item.title: Govt to slap fee on SOE loans
item.description: The Finance Division has decided to impose 0.25 per cent one-time fee on guarantees and counter-guarantees it provides against loans taken by the state-owned entities....

item.title: Govt set to halve source tax on export incentives
item.description: The Bangladesh government is set to halve the advance income tax deducted at source on export cash incentives to 5 per cent from the existing 10 per cent in the proposed national budget for FY2026-27, according to finance ministry officials......

item.title: Company to be formed to resolve bad loans: Mostaq
item.description: Bangladesh Bank governor said that a distressed asset management company would be formed to take over and resolve bad loans from banks......

item.title: Trade deficit hits $22.2b in 10 months
item.description: Bangladesh’s trade deficit widened sharply in the first ten months of the current fiscal year as import growth significantly outpaced export earnings......

item.title: BSEC removes floor price on BEXIMCO, IBBL shares
item.description: Bangladesh Securities and Exchange Commission on Monday removed floor price restrictions on shares of Bangladesh Export Import Company (BEXIMCO) and Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC after near four years......

item.title: BGB-BSF TALKS: Dhaka to highlight border killings, push-ins
item.description: Dhaka will widely discuss the border killing and push-in issues in the 57th four-day director general-level talks between the Border Guard Bangladesh and the Indian Border Security Force, which began on Monday in New Delhi, India....

item.title: BB extends three funds worth Tk 16,000cr
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has launched three new refinancing schemes worth a combined Tk 16,000 crore to ease working capital shortages in the cottage, micro, small and medium enterprise (CMSME) sector, increase agricultural production and economic activities in rural area, and promote environmentally sustainable industrial investment......

item.title: PMI witnesses faster expansion in May
item.description: The country’s Purchasing Managers’ Index in May witnessed an expansion by 8.2 points to 62.8 from the previous month, indicating a faster pace of expansion compared to the previous month, according to the latest PMI report published jointly by the Metropolitan Chamber of Commerce and Industry and Policy Exchange Bangladesh......

item.title: Extension of shopping hours until 10:00pm demanded
item.description: The Bangladesh Shop Business Owners’ Association called for shops, markets and shopping malls across the country to remain open until 10:00pm, arguing that current closing hours are hurting sales and deepening financial pressure on retailers......

item.title: BSF’S PUSH-IN BIDS: Families spend days in sun, rain without food
item.description: Scenes of people passing days in no-man’s-land of the Bangladesh-Indian border over the past few days reveal a looming humanitarian crisis fuelled by relentless push-in attempts by the Indian Border Security Force....

item.title: BCCCI to join China-South Asia Expo
item.description: A 23-member business delegation led by Bangladesh China Chamber of Commerce and Industry president Mohammad Khorshed Alam will participate in the 10th China-South Asia Expo and the 30th China Kunming Import and Export Fair 2026......

item.title: Islami Bank, BB host training for SMEs
item.description: Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC’s Noakhali Zone launched a month-long entrepreneurship development programme on Monday for the country’s promising small and medium-sized enterprises, said a press release......

item.title: GP field-level staff to receive incentives thru bKash
item.description: Grameenphone and bKash have recently signed an agreement at GP house in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: ICAB hosts confce to promote women’s leadership
item.description: The Institute of Chartered Accountants of Bangladesh, through its gender inclusion and leadership committee, hosted ‘Women in Leadership & Governance Conference 2026’ at Radisson Blu Dhaka Water Garden on Monday, said a press release......

item.title: RFL Best Buy opens showroom at Banani
item.description: RFL Best Buy has opened its 560th showroom at Banani in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: Uttara Bank signs deal with Elevate Solutions
item.description: Uttara Bank PLC has recently signed an agreement with Elevate Solutions Limited to implement a modern security operations centre powered by Microsoft Sentinel, a leading SIEM platform......

item.title: MBPLC launches trade finance analytics software
item.description: Mercantile Bank PLC has inaugurated and launched their new trade finance analytics software, a robust foreign trade-based reporting and monitoring solution, said a press release......

item.title: TECNO users to get 3 months of Google AI Plus for free
item.description: TECNO is offering eligible smartphone users three months of Google AI Plus at no additional cost, said a press release......

item.title: Govt must abandon denial strategy
item.description: DESPITE concerns over prolonged electricity disruptions affecting households and industry, the energy minister told parliament on June 7 that there were no load-shedding, only temporary interruptions. The minister’s remarks however stand in contrast to the realities on the ground across the country. During the Eid-ul-Adha holidays, people from Khulna, Barishal, Rangpur to......

item.title: Govt must align fiscal policy with green energy ambitions
item.description: THIS is concerning that Bangladesh’s transition to renewable energy is being obstructed by not only a lack of investment and policy support but also a fiscal regime that actively favours fossil fuels. A recent Centre for Policy Dialogue study says that the transition to renewable energy faces ‘fiscal discrimination’. The study, unveiled on June 7, finds that fossil fuel-based......

item.title: Fix health care where it begins
item.description: A MOTHER in Kurigram should not have to travel to Dhaka for treatment that ought to be available at a nearby health centre. Yet every day, thousands of Bangladeshis make similar journeys. They crowd into buses, borrow money from relatives, and spend days away from work and family simply to access services that should exist much closer to home......

item.title: Workplace toxicity and office politics
item.description: IN CONTEMPORARY Bangladesh, young professionals are equipped with higher education, digital literacy and global exposure. Many young employees enter the workforce with dreams of innovation and career growth. Yet behind the glossy narratives of corporate success lies a troubling reality: workplace toxicity and office politics increasingly shape the professional lives......

item.title: Personal triumph, not strategic victory
item.description: THE election of Bangladesh’s foreign minister, Dr Khalilur Rahman, as president of the 81st session of the United Nations General Assembly has been celebrated across official circles as a landmark diplomatic success. It is only the second time in Bangladesh’s history that a national has secured the position, the first being Humayun Rashid Choudhury in 1986. The......

item.title: Decoding upcoming local government elections
item.description: NATIONAL elections dominate political discussion in Bangladesh and local elections often get somewhat overshadowed. Yet, it is these grassroots elections that determine who is responsible for building local roads, managing waste, supervising sanitation services, ensuring safe water, responding to emergencies and providing many of the public services that......

item.title: IUB top private univ in Bangladesh in Research ranking
item.description: Independent University, Bangladesh ranked the top private university in Bangladesh in the Research.com best universities in Bangladesh 2025/2026 ranking....

item.title: AC technician dies after falling from sixth floor in Dhaka
item.description: A man died after falling from the sixth floor of a building while installing an air conditioner in the capital’s Elephant Road area on Sunday night....

item.title: 50 hurt in clash between UP chair, acting chair in Habiganj
item.description: More than fifty people, including four policemen, were injured in a two-hour-long clash between the supporters of Awami League-backed chairman and BNP-backed acting chairman of Baraiuri union parishad at Baniachong upazila in Habiganj on Monday afternoon....

item.title: Police inspector withdrawn after alleged bribery video goes viral
item.description: The district police in Rajshahi on Monday withdrew Bagmara police station inspector Kamalesh Das after a video allegedly showing him accepting a bribe went viral on social media....

item.title: 65 more hospitalised with dengue in 24hrs
item.description: The Directorate General of Health Services on Monday said that sixty-five more people were admitted to hospitals across the country with dengue infection during the past 24 hours....

item.title: Gandharbapur plant to supply 50cr litre water to Dhaka daily: Fakhrul
item.description: Local government, rural development and cooperatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Monday said that once the Gandharbapur Water Treatment Plant became operational, 50 crore litres of treated water from the Meghna River would be supplied to Dhaka city daily....

item.title: Aedes breeding risk high in Rajshahi city
item.description: The findings of a survey, carried out in May by the Directorate General of Health Services, have raised concerns over a possible increase in dengue transmission during the coming monsoon season in Rajshahi city....

item.title: Launch of ocean satellite ground station at CU today
item.description: Chittagong University held a press conference on Monday on the occasion of the inauguration of the China-Bangladesh Ocean Satellite Ground Station for Marine Remote Sensing (Satellite Ocean Observation and Data Innovation Center) on the CU campus....

item.title: Stop job cuts in garment, other factories: labour leaders
item.description: Labour leaders on Monday at a protest rally in Dhaka called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to take steps to stop the job cuts of workers in garment and other factories across the country....

item.title: One Minute cinema festival promotes environmental content
item.description: Dheki Projozona organised a One Minute Environmental Cinema Festival on Monday at the Chitrashala Auditorium of Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy to promote environmental content....

item.title: BUET hosts inauguration ceremony of ‘exposing hidden heat’
item.description: Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology on Monday hosted the inauguration ceremony of a project titled Exposing Hidden Heat: Mapping Heat-Driven Health Disparities and Advancing Policy Implications for Climate-Resilient Urban Development in Dhaka, Bangladesh....

item.title: MOTALIB PLAZA SHOP OWNERS’ ASSOC: No action against ‘grabbing’ of convener post
item.description: The police neither conducted any investigation nor took any action over the past three weeks regarding traders’ allegations that Khandaker Akhter Hamid Khan Paban, son of former Bangladesh Nationalist Party secretary general Khandaker Delwar Hossain, had grabbed the post of convener of the capital’s Motalib Plaza Shop Owners’ Association without an election....

item.title: Rainfall likely to increase over next 5 days: BMD
item.description: Bangladesh Meteorological Department has predicted that rainfall is likely to increase across the country over the next five days commencing 9:00am on Monday....

item.title: Mauritius to reopen labour market for Bangladeshis, says minister
item.description: Bangladesh’s labour market in Mauritius is set to reopen after the labour ministers of the two countries agreed to advance a bilateral recruitment framework during talks on the sidelines of the 114th International Labour Conference in Geneva in Switzerland....

item.title: Patients suffer as intern doctors continue strike
item.description: Intern doctors at public and private medical college hospitals across the country continued their indefinite work abstention for the second consecutive day on Monday, pressing a six-point demand that includes an increase in allowances and the cancellation of the health ministry’s decision on FCPS training....

item.title: RMG workers block Dhaka roads for hours
item.description: Traffic choked Dhaka roads for up to two hours on Monday, causing immense suffering for commuters, as garments workers from different factories took to the streets over sudden layoffs and unpaid wages at Mohakhali and Rampura....

item.title: CTG CHILD RAPE: Charge hearing today
item.description: The Chattogram Metropolitan Violence against Children Prevention Tribunal on Monday accepted the charge sheet in the case of raping a four-year-old girl in the Bakalia area of the port city on May 21....

item.title: UK, France, Germany back Zelensky’s call for Putin meeting
item.description: Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky received the backing of the leaders of the UK, France and Germany in calling for direct ceasefire talks between Russia and Ukraine, according to a joint statement issued following defence talks in London on Sunday....

item.title: 3rd death anniv of Serajul Alam Khan today
item.description: The third anniversary of the death of Serajul Alam Khan, one of the key organisers of the country’s War of Independence, will be observed today....

item.title: Abul Barkat yet to be released despite SC bail
item.description: The police have sought permission from the Dhaka Chief Metropolitan Magistrate Court to show detained economist and former Janata Bank chairman Professor Abul Barkat arrested in a fresh case filed over a murder during the 2024 July uprising....

item.title: TIB report on law and order based on newspaper clippings, not investigation: Salahuddin
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Monday dismissed the recent Transparency International Bangladesh report on the country’s law and order situation, saying it was based on newspaper clippings rather than independent investigations and did not reflect official crime statistics....

item.title: 35 killed in Philippines quake
item.description: A 7.8-magnitude earthquake in the southern Philippines on Monday killed at least 35 people, according to provincial authorities, after toppling buildings and sparking tsunami warnings across the region....

item.title: Two electrocuted during grave digging
item.description: Two people were electrocuted while digging a grave at Neamotpur of Molagram union under Kasba upazila in Brahmanbaria on Monday at about 11.00 am....

item.title: Newlywed man kills wife, attempts suicide
item.description: Newlywed husband Sujon Hossain, 22, an alleged drug addict, tried to commit suicide after killing his wife Samina Akhter Shammi, 20, at Sheikhhati Tamaltala on the outskirts of Jashore town early Monday, locals and police said....

item.title: Meherpur University gets new VC
item.description: Islamic University’s Arabic language and literature department professor M Tozammel Hossain has been appointed as the new vice-chancellor of Meherpur University....

item.title: 20 MPs exit from Trinamool Congress plan to form a separate bloc
item.description: The rebellion within the Trinamool Congress reached the national capital on Monday, with several of its MPs reportedly holding a meeting at the residence of Union Minister and Bharatiya Janata Party's West Bengal observer, Bhupendra Yadav, according to The Hindu....

item.title: Women, children expose to growing risk in cyberspace in Bangladesh: experts
item.description: As Bangladesh continues its rapid digital transformation, women and children are increasingly falling prey to cyberspace blackmailing. ...

item.title: Headless body recovered from canal in Barguna
item.description: The police recovered the headless body of a missing businessman from a canal under Barguna Sadar in the district on Monday....

item.title: GK project redesigns pumping system to cope with falling Padma water levels
item.description: Bangladesh’s largest surface irrigation scheme, the Ganges-Kobadak Irrigation Project, has undertaken a major redesign of its pumping system to enable water withdrawal from the Padma River at lower water levels, amid concerns over dry-season flow reductions linked to upstream diversion....

item.title: Coast Guard hands over 91 fishermen detained in India to families
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard on Monday handed over 91 fishermen detained in India to their family members along with their fishing boats at Coast Guard base Mongla in Bagerhat....
